'''Mitch Clark''' (born {{birth date|1993|3|30|df=yes}}) is a [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]], [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press championship]], and the [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played one game at the [[York City Knights]] in 2017 on dual registration from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], before moving to [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the top flight. He spent time on loan from the [[Castleford Tigers|Tigers]] in 2018. He played one game for [[Halifax RLFC]] on dual registration in 2019. Clark also played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Clark was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

He is of New Zealand heritage, however he was born in England while his father, [[Trevor Clark (rugby league)|Trevor Clark]] was playing for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] after stints with [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]]. He followed in his father's footsteps in 2016 when he débuted for the [[Bradford Bulls]] against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Early career==
Clark played in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup under-20s competition]] in 2012 with the Penrith Panthers before the competition was renamed the Holden Cup in 2013. He played in the 2013 Holden Cup Grand final for the Penrith Panthers over the New Zealand Warriors and scored a try in the 30-42 victory.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

He represented the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2013 against the Junior Kangaroos beaten 38-26 at WIN Jubilee Oval Kograh on 13 October.

==Playing career==
===Doncaster===
Clark spent 2015 with [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]].{{citation needed|date=October 2017}}

===Bradford Bulls===
Clark joined the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2016 but did not feature in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]].

He played in Round 2 ([[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]) to Round 12 ([[London Broncos]]). Mitch played in Round 14 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Round 18 ([[Batley Bulldogs]]). He featured in the Championship Shield in Game 3 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]) to the Final ([[Sheffield Eagles]]). Clark played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th Round ([[Dewsbury Rams]]). He scored against [[Workington Town]] (2 tries), [[Leigh Centurions]] (1 try), [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] (1 try) and [[Dewsbury Rams]] (1 try).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Mitch Clark signed a 1-year deal with the Hull Kingston Rovers for the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/hull-kr-confirm-captures-of-bradford-bulls-duo-mitch-clark-and-danny-addy/story-29819177-detail/story.html|title=Hull KR confirm captures of Bradford Bulls duo Mitch Clark and Danny Addy|work=[[Hull Daily Mail]]|date=18 October 2016|accessdate=6 December 2016}}{{Dead link|date=November 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Clark featured in Round 1 ([[Bradford Bulls]]) to Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]).

===Castleford Tigers===
In October 2017 he signed for the [[Castleford Tigers]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/confirmed-mitch-clark-leaves-hull-651276|title=Confirmed - Mitch Clark leaves Hull KR to join Castleford Tigers|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|date=19 October 2017|accessdate=8 December 2017}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
Clark moved to the [[Wigan Warriors]] ahead of the [[2020 Super League season]].{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
On 10 Jun 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Jun 2021|accessdate=11 Jun 2021|title=Newcastle snap up Mitch Clark and Kyle Trout|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/newcastle-snap-up-mitch-clark-and-kyle-trout/}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 7 Nov 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Nov 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Wigan forward Mitch Clark makes Championship move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-forward-mitch-clark-makes-championship-move/}}</ref>

===Baroudeurs de Pia XIII===
On 24 Oct 2022 it was reported that he had signed for [[Baroudeurs de Pia XIII]] in the [[Elite One Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Examiner.co.uk|date=24 Oct 2022|accessdate=25 Oct 2022|title=Catalans Dragons centre and former Wigan Warriors prop set for French league move|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/dean-whare-mitch-clark-pia-25342070.amp}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 31 Oct 2023 it was reported that he will join [[Sheffield Eagles]] for the 2024 season on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Oct 2023|title=Former Super League prop Mitch Clark finds new club in Championship on two-year deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-prop-mitch-clark-finds-new-club-in-championship-sheffied-eagles}}</ref>
===York Knights===
On 26 February 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Sheffield Eagles]] to join Championship rivals [[York Knights]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Feb 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Mitch Clark set for switch to Championship rivals after Sheffield Eagles exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mitch-clark-set-for-switch-to-championship-rivals-after-sheffield-eagles-exit}}</ref>
